g.data-narration: To store custom objects in hash-based collections like HashSet or HashMap, you must properly override equals and hashCode methods. These methods define object equality and hash value calculation.
g.text: public boolean equals(Object obj) - Define object equality
public int hashCode() - Calculate hash value for bucketing

g.data-narration: The equals and hashCode contract requires that if two objects are equal according to equals, they must have the same hash code. The reverse is not required but desirable for performance.
g.text: ✓ If a.equals(b) is true, then a.hashCode() == b.hashCode() MUST be true
✓ If a.hashCode() == b.hashCode(), a.equals(b) MAY be true (hash collision)

g.data-narration: For sorted collections like TreeSet or TreeMap, objects must implement Comparable for natural ordering or provide a Comparator for custom ordering. The compareTo method defines sort order.
g.text: Implement Comparable<T> with int compareTo(T other)
OR provide Comparator<T> to the collection constructor

g.data-narration: Example Student class shows proper implementation with equals checking roll number equality, hashCode using Objects.hash utility, and compareTo for natural ordering by roll number.
g.text: private int rollNo; private String name;
public boolean equals(Object o) { return rollNo == ((Student)o).rollNo; }
public int compareTo(Student s) { return Integer.compare(rollNo, s.rollNo); }
